steps.m: from/out of (shows تُرَابٍ is the source material)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows تُرَابٍ (dust/soil) is what Allah created humans from. It's like saying 'made from' in English.

steps.m: to flow or drip in small amounts

steps.m: from a drop (connected to مِن before it)

steps.m: then/after that (connecting خَلَقَكُم with what follows)
steps.n: This word connects two parts of Allah's creation process, showing He created humans first from dust, then from a drop of fluid. The ثُمَّ here shows these events happened in sequence with a time gap between them.

steps.n: Connected the root letters together

steps.m: from (showing the origin/source of خَلَقَكُم - your creation)

This is a special word that shows an exception - it tells us that something is left out from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it's connecting to the word تَضَعُ (she delivers) to show that no female delivers except with His knowledge.

steps.n: Basic meaning of knowledge
Changed from basic root meaning 'know' into the noun 'knowledge' by adding the vowel marks kasra (ِ) and sukoon (ْ)

steps.m: with His knowledge (referring to Allah's knowledge)
steps.n: Added the possessive pronoun هِ (His) to show this is Allah's knowledge. The ۦ after هِ is just a pronunciation mark.

Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase to the previous statement about creation

Adding يُ at start and ُ at end makes it passive present tense - showing someone receiving the action

steps.m: from (connecting تُرَابٍ 'dust' to خَلَقَكُم 'He created you')
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects how Allah created humans from dust.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: one who is made to live long
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start changes the verb into a noun describing someone affected by the action

This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative statement with the previous phrase. This negates the verb تَضَعُ (gives birth) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: is being decreased (from عُمُرِهِۦٓ - his age)
steps.n: Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show this is happening to something rather than something doing it. This word is connected to عُمُرِهِۦٓ (his age) which is what's being decreased.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word مُّعَمَّرٍ (one given long life) to the verb يُعَمَّرُ (is given long life), showing that no one from among those given long life has their life changed except with Allah's knowledge.
This pattern makes the root into a noun meaning 'the period of living'
The damma vowels turn the root into a noun meaning the period of living
Adding kasra shows this word connects to the next word
Adding هِ shows the lifespan belongs to him
The ۦٓ after هِ shows this is elongated when reading
This is a particle that shows exception. In this ayah, it connects to the word بِعِلْمِهِۦ (with His knowledge) to show that nothing happens except with Allah's knowledge.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that something is recorded in a book/record.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

This is a preposition that shows something is easy or light upon/over Allah. In this ayah it connects the word يَسِيرٌ (easy) with ٱللَّهِ (Allah) to show that the previous matters mentioned are easy for Allah to do.
Added emphasis by doubling the ل letter

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to what comes before)

Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past

steps.m: he was (describes عٰقِبَةُ - the end result)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to create an intensive form showing complete/perfect knowledge. This form is often used to describe Allah's perfect attributes.

steps.m: all-knowing (describing اللَّهُ as the predicate of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end because this word comes after كَانَ (was) as its predicate, telling us what Allah was/is.
Added ي between د and ر and put ِ (kasra) on د to show this describes someone with complete power

steps.m: powerful (describing Allah in كَانَ عَلِيمًا قَدِيرًا - He was All-Knowing, All-Powerful)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes 'was' (كَانَ) - like saying 'He was powerful'
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations. It's different from إِنْ which is used for likely conditions.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This word introduces a hypothetical situation that connects to the next phrase about Allah taking people to account.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show interaction between two parties
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels. Allah is doing the action of holding accountable.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is the subject of يُؤَاخِذُ)
steps.n: Changed to the proper name Allah, who is doing the action of taking to account (يُؤَاخِذُ) in this verse

steps.m: people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
steps.n: The root أنس meaning 'to be social' becomes نَاس meaning 'people' by dropping the first letter and adding a long 'aa' sound. This makes it a noun for humans who are social beings.
Adding a shadda (doubling mark) on the ن adds emphasis to the word for 'people'
Added ٱل to point to specific people

steps.m: the people (emphasized) (object of يُؤَاخِذُ - takes to task)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يُؤَاخِذُ (takes to task)
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. It refers to the things that people earned/did in كَسَبُوا (they earned/did).
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is about doing something 'with' or 'because of' what people earned/did.

steps.m: to earn or gain something through effort

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past

steps.m: they earned (what they did in بِمَا)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people did this action. This word refers back to what was mentioned in بِمَا (what they did)
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that connects to the verb كَسَبُوا (they earned/did). Together they mean 'what they earned/did'.

steps.m: to leave something behind or abandon it
he left/abandoned (what Allah would leave on مَا تَرَكَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is part of مَا تَرَكَ (what He would leave) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is positioned above or on top of something else
The alif at the end is written with a small mark (ٰ) to show it's being stretched slightly in pronunciation. This connects it smoothly to the next word ظَهْرِهَا (its back).
Changes from verb 'to appear' into noun meaning 'back'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هَا to show this refers to something feminine (the earth) mentioned earlier
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to the word دَآبَّةٍ (creature) to show 'of creatures' or 'any creature'.
The doubled middle letter shows this is happening with intensity or repeatedly
The ة makes this a noun meaning 'a creature that moves on earth'
Added dots under the last letter (ٍ) because it comes after مِن, which makes it mean 'any creature' instead of just 'creature'
This is a basic particle that shows contrast between ideas. The alif above the ل (لٰ) helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: but, however (contrasts with previous statement about Allah taking people to task)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting idea with the previous statement. This shows that even though Allah could punish people immediately (in the previous part), He instead gives them time.
Added shadda to show strong or intense delaying
Added يُ at start to show it's happening now/future. The أ changes to ؤ because it comes after يُ.

steps.m: he delays them strongly (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being delayed. Allah (اللَّهُ) is doing the delaying.
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. Used to connect actions or things to their targets, like saying 'going to the store' in English.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation of regular alif. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word. This is just a pronunciation mark showing that we stretch the sound a little bit when reading.
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and form a noun meaning a specific time limit or term

steps.m: an appointed time/term (being acted upon by إِلَىٰٓ)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the preposition إِلَىٰٓ (to/until) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and doubled middle letter to show something was given a name (passive form)
Added ى at the end to turn it into a noun form
Added shadda on initial م and tanween to show this is indefinite. In this ayah it refers to an appointed time/term that Allah has set.
This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects to the word جَآءَ (came) to show 'when it came'.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else. It's like saying 'and then when' or 'so when' in English.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he came'
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming

steps.m: to delay, postpone, or set a deadline

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowels to form a noun meaning the time or deadline itself. This word is the subject of جَآءَ (came) in this ayah.
Added هُمْ at the end to show this deadline belongs to or relates to them
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain

steps.m: then indeed/for surely (connects to يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ - He delays them)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. Here it connects to how Allah delays people's punishment.
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُؤَاخِذُ - takes to task)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence. The word يُؤَاخِذُ means 'takes to task' and Allah is what's being acted on.
This root represents the basic idea of existing or being in a state
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows something that happened before.

steps.m: he was (describes the state of بَصِيرًۢا)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word connects to بَصِيرًۢا (All-Seeing) to tell us about Allah's attribute.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by or connected to something else
Adds the preposition ب meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with His servants (referring to Allah's servants)
steps.n: Adds the possessive pronoun 'His' referring to Allah, since Allah is mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to فَعِيل to show this is a permanent, intensive quality
Added tanween alif to show this is describing the verb كَانَ (was) in the ayah
